steps.title: Analyze the sentence structure
steps.content: The subject is “Paintings by the renowned twentieth-century US [blank]” and the predicate is “were featured in Artist to Artist…” The blank must complete the noun phrase with a head noun and an appositive that names the two specific artists. Because the names identify which artists — essential information — the appositive is restrictive and takes no commas.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and pick the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘artists Thomas Hart Benton, and Jackson Pollack,’: The comma before and implies a three-item list with a missing third element, and the trailing comma is unmatched. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘artists: Thomas Hart Benton and Jackson Pollack,’: A colon cannot separate a noun from its appositive, and the trailing comma has nothing to close. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘artists Thomas Hart Benton and Jackson Pollack’: The names flow directly as a restrictive appositive with no punctuation — correct for essential identifying information. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘artists, Thomas Hart Benton and Jackson Pollack’: A comma before a restrictive appositive is incorrect; only non-restrictive appositives are set off by commas. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
